text: Code refactoring is the process of improving the internal structure of existing code without changing its behavior. Instead of adding new features, developers focus on making the code cleaner and easier to understand. For example, they might break a long function into smaller ones, rename unclear variables, or remove duplicate code. This increases readability and helps others work with the code more easily. Refactoring also reduces technical debt, which is the extra work caused by messy or rushed code. By regularly cleaning up code, teams can avoid future problems and maintain a healthy, manageable project over time.
